rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GARRETT VS. GWENDOLYN MCCRAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce The Stipulated Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 For Judgment And Order Compelling Performance Forthwith Declaration Of Amanda L Ebey In Support Of Motion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS GWENDOLYN MCCRAY and DONALD GARRETT'S Motion To Enforce The Stipulated Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 is continued to April 9, 2021 for Plaintiff to complete payment under the terms of the Stipulation. No later than April 7, 2021 Plaintiff to file a declaration confirming payment. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN M FRANKLIN VS. UNION AND POLK LP. A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2021 line 2. rulings.Case Title: OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC ET AL VS. DONS HOTEL OWNER LP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT DONS HOTEL OWNER LP's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is continued to April 16, 2021 on Court's own motion. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. JAVONTA LYKES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT JAVONTA LYKES' DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on termination of license. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Rent Ordinance applies to licensees. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JRM FINANCIAL, LLC VS. OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANTS OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC and TONY GARNICKI's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party improperly raises issues of fact on the demurrer contradicting express allegations in the complaint that defendants are not tenants. Moving party further advances for the first time in the reply an argument that foreclosed upon owner is a tenant at sufferance subject to SFRO protections. This argument, having been advanced in the reply, is not considered. However, the Court notes that it is not supported by any authority. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JRM FINANCIAL, LLC VS. OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANTS OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC and TONY GARNICKI's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Court will not consider declarations in support and in opposition to the demurrer. Request for judicial notice of Notice of Trustee Sale is granted. Demurrer raises issues of fact that cannot be determined on a demurrer, i.e. parcel number. Furthermore moving party improperly raises issues of fact contradicting express allegations in the complaint that defendants are not tenants. Moving party further advances for the first time in the reply an argument that foreclosed upon owner is a tenant at sufferance subject to SFRO protections. This argument, having been advanced in the reply, is not considered. However, the Court notes that it is not supported by any authority. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
